Jordan Blake: Quick thread 1: Partner wants a structured private-offer waterfall (commit bands + step-down renewals) and expects to be able to on-board SI activation early. Ops/legal/finance need rules.

Aisha (Finance): Credits = contra-revenue per accounting. we require a hard cap per customer and global ceiling for exposure. propose cap = min($125k, 12% of committed ARR). will model sensitivity.

Ava Chen: Quick sync: EdgeForge wants to ship a signed upload flow for device-metering to reduce reconciliation noise. They9ll push gzipped CSVs to our S3 intake and POST a manifest + sig to our webhook.
Diego Morales: That sounds fine. We need an explicit hash algorithm (sha256) and a manifest format. Also agree on timezones and clock skew handling for device timestamps.
Priya Shah: Requirements from finance from last quarter: we must be able to run a reconciliation delta report per SKU and per customer daily, and mark any >1% variance as a P1 for manual review.
Roberto Lin: Proposed manifest (example):
```
{
 "files": [
 {"name":"usage-2026-03-18.gz","sha256":"<hex>","rows":43210}
 ],
 "partner_id":"edgeforge-001",
 "generated_at":"2026-03-18T08:00:00Z"
}
```
Signature is over the raw manifest bytes.

text:
Rohit Singh: Heads-up: prepping the partner ops section for next week's QBR. Need to nail tier movement triggers + MDF emergency rules.
Taylor Nguyen: Ok — propose we codify three movement triggers: 1) Upgrade: partner-sourced ARR > $140k over trailing 90 days AND gen pipeline conversion > 2%. 2) Downgrade: < 35% of committed pipeline closed (or formally stalled) for two quarters. 3) Fast-track upgrade (for launches): forecasted incremental ARR > $200k within 6 months with partner exec sponsor.
Marta Lopez: For downgrades, let's not penalize deals stalled by the customer's procurement/legal delays. Tag `procurement-delay` or `legal-hold` to exclude.
Taylor Nguyen: Agree. Add a 45-day grace window post-product-launch.
Jules Martinez: MDF caps? Finance wants fixed ceilings. My draft: Tier A $180k/yr, Tier B $60k/yr, Tier C $15k/yr. Emergency top-up of +40% one-time with approvals.
Ava C...

title: #partnerships: 2391110000

Taylor Nguyen: S...
text:
Taylor Nguyen: Heads up — prepping the Q partner review next week. I want us to finalize the trigger mechanics we'll present: when a partner moves between tiers, and the emergency co-invest (aka escape hatch) rules for MDF spend.
Rohit Singh: 👍 Can you summarise the proposed triggers? Numbers help.
Taylor Nguyen: Short version: 1) Tier upgrade if partner ARR sourced > $150k over trailing 90 days and lead gen conversion > 2.5%. 2) Tier downgrade if partner activity < 30% of their committed pipeline for two consecutive quarters. 3) Emergency co-invest unlock when a partner hits a one-time product-market event (launch, large SI deal) and forecasts > $250k incremental ARR in 6 months.
Jules Martinez: Those look reasonable. For downgrade rule: do we count paused deals? sometimes opportunities sit in pipeline but stall due to customer procurement.
Ava Chen: Good point — propose we exclude ...

Maya Patel: Heads up — Vanguard Compute just returned a countersigned NDA and want to kick off a short early-access bench/ops loop next week. They requested a 1-node sandbox, telemetry access, and a short press embargo.

Diego Ruiz: Great. Do we have a data transfer plan? S3 or SFTP? Also need to confirm marketplace packaging won't block timeline.

Maya Patel: They prefer SFTP for initial drops, will open an S3 endpoint later. They also want a 30-day embargo on any external announcements. @Priya Natarajan can you handle the addendum?

Priya Natarajan: On it. I'll push the NDA addendum today and aim for countersign by EOD tomorrow. Need the Vanguard contact (email) to cc legal.

text:

Quick notes: Zenith says min_commit_units=8 or min_monthly_commit=$12,000; cross_region_egress_pct=0.8% and a fulfillment surcharge = 1.0% + $500 per TB after the first 2TB. Aurora replied: marketplace_fee_pct=3.0% flat, no hard min if you sign a partner MOU but there's a $2k onboarding fee for <5-device pools. Vega replied yesterday with a CSV but flagged variable per-region egress tiers.

text:
Nora Vasquez: Heads-up: partner requested a lightweight Terraform "edge" kit for Marketplace that supports quick deploy on partner-managed clouds (AWS/GCP) and an optional GPU path. Core ask: minimal variables, clear SKU mapping, and CI that can produce signed artifacts.

Ethan Park: I like minimal. Proposed variables: cloud, region, env, enable_gpu=false, gpu_profile="". If enable_gpu=true and gpu_profile empty error out. Keep networking simple: use existing VPC if provided, else create a lean subnet.

Clara Liu: For the SKU mapping, I'll add a csv in /docs/sku_matrix.csv with columns: cloud, region, recommended_sku, notes. We'll also include a tiny helper script map_sku.py that reads desired_profile and prints recommended SKU.

Nora Vasquez: Packaging needs: tar.gz with manifest.json (semver, sha256, components list) and GPG signature. Partner legal wants the signature before publis...

```hcl

}
```

text:
Priya Rao: heads up — EdgeConnect (SI) ran into a blocker during their private deployment for Acme Health. marketplace image passed basic checks but their on-prem nodes fail during node-driver init.
Ben Carter: can you paste the error/log snippet?
Priya Rao: sure — partner sent this from node0: `kernel: module 'accelerator_adapter' failed to load: version magic mismatch` and `redwood-agent: image attestation check failed: signer not trusted`.
Chris Liu: hmm looks like two separate issues: kernel module/driver ABI mismatch + image signing/trust chain. which one is blocking the go-live?
Priya Rao: both. driver prevents GPU binding, attestation blocks our marketplace install path (we enforce signed images for private deploys). EdgeConnect says their build system signs with an internal CI key not in our truststore.
Dana Ortiz: we need a short arch review with EdgeConnect + Eng Runtime + P...

Lukas Meyer: GCP partner team wants a concrete testing plan before they’ll move our submission forward. Can we align on an E2E plan for GCP marketplace: subscription → entitlement → Redwood org provisioning (staging + prod) + how we prove metering is correct?

Lukas Meyer: Drafting something like below, please poke holes / add steps:
1) In GCP marketplace, subscribe to Redwood listing (test buyer project)
2) GCP creates entitlement
3) Redwood entitlement sync job picks it up
4) User lands in console marketplace entrypoint, links/creates org
5) Entitlement verified (backend) and limits applied
6) First API key → first successful request
7) Metering dims show up in GCP billing export / internal billing reconciliation

text:
Lukas Meyer: Heads up — got the latest note from our GCP Marketplace reviewer. They’re not blocking yet, but they *will* if we don’t attach the required artifacts in the Producer Portal submission. Dropping the list here + proposed deadlines so we can assign owners.

Lukas Meyer: **GCP listing required artifacts (SaaS) — v1 checklist**
1) **Security / privacy docs (buyer-facing):**
- Security overview (shared responsibility, data handling, encryption in transit/at rest, KMS) 
- Data retention + deletion policy (defaults + configurable options)
- Audit logging overview (what events, export options)
- Compliance posture statement (SOC 2 / ISO wording ok, but no over-claims)
2) **Architecture diagrams:**
- High-level system architecture diagram (where Redwood runs vs customer)
- Data flow diagram (what data is processed/stored; where logs go)
3) **Support + SLA info:**
- Support contact ...

Chris Osei: AWS review feedback came back (round 1). Dropping the key items here + proposed owners. Please react :thumbsup: if you can take, or reply w/ an alt owner.

Chris Osei: Summary from AWS team:
1) Missing artifacts: need Security Overview doc (public URL), Data Flow Diagram (image/PDF), Support contact + SLA statement, and EULA/terms link.
2) Wording changes: remove/soften comparative claims (“best-in-class”, “#1 latency”) unless we cite a public benchmark. Also avoid “SOC2 certified” phrasing.
3) Offer structure: clarify what’s included in each SKU vs add-ons; current copy reads like Dedicated/Private are features inside Hosted.
4) Screenshots: they want at least 3 product screenshots (console, usage dashboard, audit logs) w/ captions.

text:
Maya Patel: Quick q for growth folks — we’re lining up a Feb partner push w/ Cloud marketplaces (AWS + GCP listings) + 1 co-marketed webinar. If we’re running retargeting at the same time, are we coordinating audiences/UTMs so attribution doesn’t get messy?

Ben Carter: yeah good catch. Right now retargeting plan is (1) site visitors, (2) signup-no-activation, (3) activated-no-return. If partner traffic is hitting the same landing pages, we’ll end up with mixed UTMs + multi-touch confusion.

Tyler Benson: +1. We need to decide: (a) isolate partner landing pages (no retargeting pixel audiences off them), or (b) keep pixels but enforce strict UTM taxonomy + attribution rules (first-touch vs last-touch) so we can still measure.

Maya Patel: partner team preference is to keep partner reporting clean (they’ll want “influenced pipeline” for marketplace + webinar). But I also don’t want to k...

text:
Rina Gomez: quick callout 1: marketplace leads keep slipping between our SI partners and field reps. need a short "claim" guideline.
Ethan Park: yep seen a few duplicates this week. simplest is: claim in CRM -> partners-bot confirmation -> 48h hold by claimer.
Nora Walsh: 48h hold seems fair. add requirement: claimer must add brief reason + expected next step (demo, intro, qualify).
Rina Gomez: propose formalizing: 1) SI clicks claim -> record shows SI name, timestamp, reason, next step. 2) partners-bot posts a confirmation in #partners with lead id and claimer. 3) field rep is CC'd if account is field-owned.
Ethan Park: Q: what about overlapping claims from multiple SIs? first-claim wins, others get notified and put on watcher list.
Nora Walsh: also add mapping check: if CRM has SI_primary flag, the primary SI gets notified and gets 24h exclusive right to claim before open to others....

preview:

Priya Singh: If we drop the floor we need to cap discount depth. Suggest 10% max first-ye...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — CloudPartner asked if we can lower first-year commit floor for private offers to $100k for SI-led deals. Thoughts?
Noah Patel: That would help close pilots faster. What's the risk on capacity/discounting?
Priya Singh: If we drop the floor we need to cap discount depth. Suggest 10% max first-year, with a 12-month renewal hurdle to re-evaluate.
Liam O'Neil: Also ask the SI to co-fund onboarding credits. If SI chips in $10k, we can relax floor to 75k.
Ava Chen: Good. Can we formalize as a rule: floor tiers [75k, 100k, 250k, 500k] => max discounts [6%, 8%, 12%, 18%] and SI kickers?
Priya Singh: I'm for tiers but keep a "no double-dipping" clause with marketplace promotional credits — credits shouldn't stack with seller rebates.
Noah Patel: Ops note: Dedicated capacity reservations need ~2 weeks lead time. Smaller floors might create fragmentation and hurt utilizatio...

text:
Camila Reyes: pulling together options for reverse ETL / enrichment to get PLG signals + account context into HubSpot without us building a million one-off syncs. What vendors should we evaluate + do we even want a partner here vs build in-house?

Ben Carter: +1. Context: MVP is telemetry -> analytics -> scoring svc -> HubSpot API. Works, but we keep getting asks for “enrich with firmographic / intent / billing tier / workspace rollups” and it’s turning into a mini-ETL project.

Tyler Benson: from Marketing Ops POV: reverse ETL vendor would help us keep HubSpot properties sane + manage workflows/lists. But I’m nervous about another system writing to HubSpot without clear ownership + suppression logic.

David Kim: fwiw HubSpot API limits are real (already seeing 429s). If a vendor blasts updates, we’ll be back in rate-limit hell unless we design around it.

Isabelle Laurent: also need ...

Ben Carter: dropping this here so we don’t keep re-litigating in DMs — can someone outline the AWS Marketplace *private offer* steps + what templates we need + who owns each step? Need this for the regulated ABM targets (finserv first) and co-sell motion.

Tyler Benson: yep. here’s the “happy path” checklist we’ve used before. will paste rough steps + owners, then we can turn into a one-pager.

Tyler Benson: **AWS MP Private Offer (CPPO) – steps (internal)**
1) Confirm we’re selling via AWS MP for this account (vs direct order form). Owner: AE + Partnerships.
2) Identify SKU / product listing in MP (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private). Owner: Partnerships (w/ Product).
3) Create/associate the customer opp in ACE + tag for co-sell (if we want AWS overlay). Owner: Partnerships + AE.
4) Collect inputs for offer: legal entity, billing account ID, start date, term, region constraints, pricing ...

text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up: Kestrel customer is targeting a Saturday go-live (UTC) and the SI needs an architecture sign-off before we handoff. Two immediate blockers: marketplace image rejected by signing policy, and customer legal wants explicit telemetry opt-in flow documented.

Ravi Patel: On marketplace image — can you paste the rejection log? I want to check if it's signing metadata or the provenance bundle.

Jules Park: Paste from the marketplace webhook: `status: rejected, reason: signature-mismatch, code: IMG_SIG_403`. Customer image was built by the SI with our base runtime + custom driver.

Jules Park: Repro steps we ran locally: `rsi-cli sign --key=kestrel-prod --image=si/custom-driver:v3 && rsi-cli verify --image=si/custom-driver:v3` -> verify fails with the same error.

Marcus Li: Possible cause: SI's CI re-bundled layers after signing (timestamped layers). Need to confirm their...

title: #partnerships: 1718933333
preview:
Maya Lopez: Hey team, kick-off for a small joint perf pilot with CascadeCompute. Goal: optimize perf/W and validate throughput stability at 2.5k toks/s on 12k context. Also want a short partner-ready blurb if results look good.
Ben Carter: Sounds good. Measurement plan idea: p50/p95 latency, sustained throughput ove...
text:
Maya Lopez: Hey team, kick-off for a small joint perf pilot with CascadeCompute. Goal: optimize perf/W and validate throughput stability at 2.5k toks/s on 12k context. Also want a short partner-ready blurb if results look good.
Ben Carter: Sounds good. Measurement plan idea: p50/p95 latency, sustained throughput over 30m windows, rack-level and per-GPU power. Also capture thermal throttling events.
Anika - CascadeCompute: We can provide per-board ammeter readings and chassis thermals. Our baseline is ~2.0k toks/s @ 280W (PDU measured). We'll throw our kernel engs at it.
Maya Lopez: Nice. Keep public claims conservative. Marketing (Jules) please note: prefer relative uplift phrasing, no absolute SLA commitments.
Jules (Marketing): Got it. We want one hero perf/W vs power curve and a short callout comparing "stock" vs "tuned" configs. I'll prepare a template blurb once we have prelimina...
